message:
Fix should_compact() doctest to create actual fragmentation

The doctest was incorrectly testing compaction detection because:
- Original loop: alloc → decref in sequence for each entry
- This allows immediate slot reuse (freed slot reused by next alloc)
- Result: Pool never actually fragments, just reuses same slots

Fixed by:
- Allocate ALL entries first into a Vec, THEN free some
- Change free pattern from 50% (every other) to ~66% (every non-multiple-of-3)
- This ensures freed slots cannot be reused during allocation phase
- Pool now genuinely fragments: large capacity with many free slots

The 66% fragmentation exceeds the 50% threshold, ensuring should_compact()
returns true for the "large fragmented pool" test case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
